content_markdown:
Two identical boxes X and Y each contain an ideal gas.

In box X there are $n$ moles of the gas at temperature $T$ and pressure $P_X$. In box Y there are $2n$ moles of the gas at temperature $\frac{T}{3}$ and pressure $P_Y$.

The ratio $\frac{P_X}{P_Y}$ is

diagram_metadata.description: Two rectangular boxes labeled Box X and Box Y. Box X contains n moles, temperature T, and pressure P_X. Box Y contains 2n moles, temperature T/3, and pressure P_Y.

vibe_explanation: Using the ideal gas law $PV = nRT$, and knowing the volumes $V$ are identical for both boxes, we can express pressure as $P = \frac{nRT}{V}$. For box X, $P_X = \frac{nRT}{V}$. For box Y, $P_Y = \frac{(2n)R(T/3)}{V} = \frac{2}{3} \frac{nRT}{V}$. Taking the ratio $\frac{P_X}{P_Y} = \frac{1}{2/3} = \frac{3}{2}$.
